condition_precedents: License utilisation audit completed (usage data ≥ 90 days)
Over-provisioned tiers identified; downgrade path confirmed with vendor
ITAM tooling deployed to prevent re-provisioning creep
industry_sources: Gartner — IT Key Metrics Data: Software Asset Management 2024 (gartner.com/en/information-technology/insights/it-budget) — licence utilisation and right-sizing savings 12-30%
FinOps Foundation — State of FinOps 2025 (data.finops.org/2025-report/) — software licence cost management benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Deploy SAM (Software Asset Management) tool to scan all endpoints and servers; reconcile entitlements vs active usage over 90 days
Identify over-licensed users (named users with zero usage in 90 days) and tier mismatches (full licence where reader/viewer suffices)
Reclaim over-licensed seats before next renewal; right-size tier mix in renewal negotiation with vendor (Microsoft, Oracle, SAP, Salesforce)
Implement continuous licence monitoring; gate new seat requests through SAM tool; track licence spend per head monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Software licence spend per employee >₹80,000/year with >30% of seats showing zero or minimal usage in past 90 days
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rightsizing will yield direct licence spend reduction at next renewal

diagnostic_signals.signal: Full-access licences issued to users who only consume reports or dashboards, where cheaper viewer tier exists
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Tier rationalisation will reduce per-seat cost without impacting productivity

diagnostic_signals.signal: Multi-year licence agreements expiring within 12 months with no renegotiation underway
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Renewal window is open for rightsizing negotiation

required_data_fields: Licence entitlement data by software title, user, and tier
Active usage data (logins, feature usage) by user over 90 days
Contract renewal dates and pricing tiers by vendor
Headcount forecast for planning period
Vendor audit exposure and licence true-up obligations

condition_precedents: Cloud cost visibility tooling deployed (e.g. CloudHealth, Apptio, native dashboards)
Tagging taxonomy enforced for cost allocation by team / product / environment
Reserved instance or savings plan commitment approved by Finance
industry_sources: FinOps Foundation — State of FinOps 2025 (data.finops.org/2025-report/) — cloud cost optimisation: reserved instances and savings plans 18-40%
NASSCOM — Cloud Adoption in India Report 2024 (nasscom.in/knowledge-center/publications) — cloud spend benchmarks for Indian IT/ITES
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Enable cloud cost discovery: tag all resources by BU, project, and environment; implement cost allocation dashboard by AWS/Azure/GCP billing account
Identify top-5 cost drivers: idle/underutilised resources, oversized instances, unattached volumes, and unused reserved instances
Right-size running instances and migrate to modern SKUs; purchase reserved instances or savings plans for steady-state workloads
Implement automated shutdown for non-prod environments outside business hours; enforce budget alerts and FinOps governance monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Cloud resources with CPU/memory utilisation <20% on average representing >30% of total cloud spend
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rightsizing will reduce cloud spend without impacting workload performance

diagnostic_signals.signal: On-demand pricing paid for >60% of steady-state workloads (no reserved instances or savings plans)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Commitment purchase will reduce on-demand premium by 30-40%

diagnostic_signals.signal: Non-production environments running 24/7 with weekend and off-hours utilisation <10%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automated shutdown will reduce non-prod cloud spend by 40-60%

required_data_fields: Cloud billing data by account, service, and resource (3 months)
Resource utilisation data (CPU, memory, storage) by instance
Tagging hygiene report and resource inventory
Reserved instance and savings plan coverage and utilisation
Non-production environment inventory and usage pattern

condition_precedents: Rate card benchmarked against market (offshore, nearshore, onshore mix)
SOW vs. T&M billing ratio reviewed and optimised
Preferred supplier panel rationalised to < 5 strategic vendors
industry_sources: NASSCOM — Annual Report 2024 (nasscom.in/knowledge-center/publications) — subcontracting and delivery cost structure in India IT services
Everest Group — IT Services Pricing Trends 2024 (everestgrp.com) — rate card benchmarks for India-based delivery
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Classify all subcontractor spend: T&M vs fixed-price, offshore vs onshore, and vendor tier; calculate effective bill rate and margin by vendor
Assess Statement of Work quality: identify T&M contracts that should be fixed-price given defined scope; convert and enforce milestone billing
Rationalise vendor panel: concentrate volume in top-5 strategic partners for improved pricing and SLA leverage; exit tail vendors
Track subcontractor spend as % of project cost, effective bill rate by skill category, and vendor SLA compliance monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Subcontractor spend >35% of total project cost with >60% on T&M basis for work with defined scope
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Fixed-price conversion and bill-rate renegotiation will reduce subcontractor cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Effective bill rate for subcontractor roles 20% above internal FTE equivalent cost for similar skills
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Renegotiation or in-house replacement will reduce cost per skilled FTE

diagnostic_signals.signal: Vendor panel >30 subcontractors with bottom-half contributing <10% of project delivery
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation will improve pricing leverage and reduce vendor management overhead

required_data_fields: Subcontractor spend by vendor, project, and contract type (T&M vs fixed-price)
Bill rate by vendor and skill category vs internal FTE cost equivalent
Vendor performance data (SLA compliance, defect rate, on-time delivery)
Statement of Work quality assessment by contract
Internal bench vs subcontractor skills overlap analysis

condition_precedents: Tech debt inventory quantified (story points and BAU cost per module)
Engineering capacity ring-fenced for debt remediation (not feature work)
Architecture review board approval for platform simplification
industry_sources: McKinsey & Company — Demystifying the Real Cost of Technical Debt (mckinsey.com/capabilities/mckinsey-digital) — tech debt productivity drag and remediation ROI
Gartner — Technical Debt Management 2024 (gartner.com) — BAU cost reduction from tech debt reduction 15-40%
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Conduct tech debt inventory: assess each system for code quality (cyclomatic complexity, test coverage), operational risk, and business cost (incident rate, manual workarounds)
Prioritise debt by business impact × remediation effort matrix; build business case for top-5 debt items using incident cost and velocity impact data
Allocate dedicated tech debt sprint capacity (20% of engineering velocity); execute refactoring roadmap with measurable quality gates
Track DORA metrics (deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, MTTR) and incident cost monthly; report velocity improvement to CTO

diagnostic_signals.signal: Change failure rate >15% or MTTR >4 hours for production incidents on core systems indicating high tech debt
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Tech debt payoff will reduce incident frequency and recovery cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Engineering velocity declining or stagnating despite headcount growth due to maintenance overhead
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Tech debt is consuming engineering capacity at the expense of new capability

diagnostic_signals.signal: Incident cost (engineering time + business impact) >5% of total engineering budget annually
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Tech debt reduction ROI is positive on 18-month payback horizon

required_data_fields: Tech debt inventory by system with business impact and remediation effort estimates
DORA metrics history (deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, MTTR)
Incident log with engineering resolution time and business cost
Engineering velocity trend (story points delivered per sprint over 12 months)
System architecture assessment for highest-debt components

condition_precedents: SaaS inventory audit completed (all subscriptions catalogued)
Functional overlap map produced; retirement list approved by IT governance
Data migration plan for deprecated tools executed
industry_sources: Gartner — Market Guide for SaaS Management Platforms 2024 (gartner.com) — shadow IT elimination and SaaS consolidation savings
FinOps Foundation — State of FinOps 2025 (data.finops.org/2025-report/) — platform rationalisation benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build application portfolio inventory: map all active applications to business function, technology, cost, and user base; flag functional duplicates
Apply rationalization criteria (keep, consolidate, migrate, retire) to each application; develop migration sequencing based on dependencies and risk
Execute migration in waves starting with lowest-risk applications; decommission retired systems and reclaim licences
Track application count, annual maintenance cost per app, and licence spend reduction monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Application portfolio >200 applications with >30% estimated as candidates for consolidation or retirement
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Rationalization will reduce maintenance cost and complexity

diagnostic_signals.signal: Annual maintenance and support cost for legacy applications >40% of total IT spend
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Migrating off legacy will redirect spend to value-adding capability

diagnostic_signals.signal: Functional duplicate applications in >3 business domains (e.g., multiple CRMs, 2+ ERP instances)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Consolidation to single platform will reduce per-function technology cost

required_data_fields: Application inventory with business owner, user count, annual cost, and age
Functional overlap assessment (capability map vs application coverage)
Technical debt and supportability rating by application
Migration complexity and estimated effort by application
Contract terms and licence obligations for applications under consideration

condition_precedents: AI tool procurement approved (GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Gemini Code, etc.)
Developer productivity baseline established (velocity, commit throughput)
Training and change management programme executed for engineering org
industry_sources: WEF — Future of Jobs Report 2025 (weforum.org/publications/the-future-of-jobs-report-2025/) — AI productivity gains in technology roles
McKinsey & Company — The Economic Potential of Generative AI (mckinsey.com/capabilities/mckinsey-digital) — 10-30% developer productivity improvement from AI coding assistants
NASSCOM — Generative AI Readiness in Indian IT 2024 (nasscom.in/knowledge-center/publications)

execution_playbook.step: Identify top-5 GenAI use cases by engineering function: code generation (Copilot), test automation, documentation, code review, and incident summarisation
Pilot with volunteer engineering squads; measure code acceptance rate, PR cycle time, and defect density vs non-pilot baseline
Scale proven use cases organisation-wide: standardise tool choice, establish prompt library, and integrate into IDE and CI/CD pipeline
Track engineering throughput (features delivered per sprint), time-to-merge, and GenAI tool adoption rate monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Developer time on boilerplate code, test writing, and documentation >40% of sprint capacity
diagnostic_signals.confirms: GenAI code assistance will free developer time for higher-value engineering work

diagnostic_signals.signal: Engineering throughput (features per sprint) flat or declining despite headcount growth
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Productivity augmentation tool will improve output per engineer

diagnostic_signals.signal: Existing GenAI tool licences (Copilot, etc.) paid but active usage <40% of eligible developers
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Driving adoption of existing tools will improve ROI without incremental cost

required_data_fields: Engineering time allocation by activity type (feature dev, testing, docs, review, bugs)
GenAI tool adoption data (active users vs licences) by tool and team
Engineering throughput metrics (velocity, PR cycle time, deployment frequency)
Test coverage and defect density baseline data
Cost per engineer (fully loaded) by band and location

condition_precedents: Remote / hybrid work policy formalised and board-approved
Office utilisation data collected (badge scans / desk booking ≥ 3 months)
Lease break clauses or upcoming renewal windows identified
industry_sources: JLL India — India Office Market Report 2024 (jll.com/en-in/research/india-office-market-report) — office space utilisation rates post-pandemic
NASSCOM — IT/ITES Work-from-Home Policy Benchmarks 2024 (nasscom.in/knowledge-center/publications) — remote work adoption in Indian IT sector
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Measure actual office utilisation using badge data, Wi-Fi heatmap, or booking system; establish utilisation baseline by building and floor
Define remote-first policy aligned with engineering productivity data; set target seat-to-employee ratio (1:1.5 for hybrid, 1:2 for remote-first)
Identify consolidation and exit opportunities at next lease break or expiry; negotiate surrender or sublease for excess space
Track cost-per-seat, utilisation rate, and real estate cost as % of payroll quarterly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Average office utilisation <55% of peak capacity post-implementation of hybrid work policy
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Space consolidation and sublease will reduce real estate cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Real estate cost >8% of total operating cost vs IT/ITES peer benchmark of <5% in remote-first models
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Real estate rationalisation will materially improve margin

diagnostic_signals.signal: Engineering team productivity (output per developer) equal or higher for remote vs office developers in prior assessment
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Remote-first model will not compromise engineering output while reducing facility cost

required_data_fields: Office occupancy data by floor and building (badge, Wi-Fi, booking system)
Engineering team distribution (office vs remote vs hybrid) by headcount
Real estate cost per sq ft and per seat by location
Lease terms, break-clause dates, and sublease market rates
Engineering productivity data by location and work model

condition_precedents: TCO analysis completed for bespoke vs. COTS alternatives
Engineering capacity freed from maintenance of proprietary systems
Vendor selection and contract for COTS replacement finalised
industry_sources: Gartner — Build vs. Buy Decision Framework 2024 (gartner.com/en/information-technology) — TCO analysis for build vs. buy in enterprise software
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Inventory all custom-built applications: business function, cost to build, annual maintenance, and strategic differentiation assessment
Apply build/buy/borrow decision framework: evaluate commercial alternatives for non-differentiating capabilities; calculate TCO over 5 years
Vendor market scan for shortlisted 'buy' candidates; negotiate proof-of-concept or pilot agreements before full commitment
Execute migration for validated 'buy' decisions; decommission custom build; track engineering capacity released and TCO savings

diagnostic_signals.signal: Engineering teams spending >30% of capacity on maintaining non-differentiating internal tools vs building customer-facing features
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Replacing with commercial alternatives will free engineering capacity for value-creation

diagnostic_signals.signal: Custom-built capability available as proven SaaS at <50% of annual maintenance cost of internal equivalent
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Buy decision will reduce cost and shift maintenance risk to vendor

diagnostic_signals.signal: Custom application defect rate or downtime significantly above commercial equivalent benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Commercial alternative will improve quality alongside cost reduction

required_data_fields: Custom application inventory with annual maintenance cost and engineering headcount
Business capability map with strategic differentiation classification
Commercial vendor alternatives and indicative pricing for shortlisted capabilities
5-year TCO comparison model for build vs buy by application
Engineering capacity allocation by application and feature type

condition_precedents: Engineering maturity assessment completed (manual testing > 30% of release cycle)
Automated test coverage target set (> 70% unit / integration)
DevOps toolchain selected and infrastructure provisioned
industry_sources: NASSCOM — DevOps Maturity in India IT 2024 (nasscom.in/knowledge-center/publications) — CI/CD automation adoption benchmarks
Gartner — DevOps Platform Market Guide 2024 (gartner.com) — deployment frequency and cost reduction benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Baseline DORA metrics and SDLC process: measure lead time, deployment frequency, change failure rate, and MTTR for all production services
Implement CI/CD pipeline automation for top-5 critical services: automated testing, container build, and deployment gates
Automate infrastructure provisioning (IaC using Terraform/Pulumi) and environment management; standardise on container orchestration (Kubernetes)
Track DORA metrics monthly; set targets for deployment frequency (daily) and change failure rate (<5%); reward teams against metric improvement

diagnostic_signals.signal: Deployment frequency <1/week for production services where business needs faster release cycle
diagnostic_signals.confirms: CI/CD pipeline automation will dramatically increase deployment frequency

diagnostic_signals.signal: Lead time from code commit to production >2 weeks due to manual testing and approval gates
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Pipeline automation will reduce lead time and engineering opportunity cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Infrastructure provisioning takes >2 days due to manual ticketing and configuration
diagnostic_signals.confirms: IaC and automated provisioning will reduce developer waiting time and improve throughput

required_data_fields: DORA metrics baseline (deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, MTTR)
Current CI/CD pipeline maturity assessment by service
Infrastructure provisioning time and method (manual vs automated) by environment
Engineering time allocation: feature work vs operational and deployment overhead
Cloud and container platform capabilities and current adoption level

source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that directionally support each lever's savings thesis. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.
